content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Federico Murillo 
52-256 Calle Camacho 
Coachella, CA 
92236 
Dear Mr. Murillo: 
November 21, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-584 
You have requested advice regarding how many Coachella City 
staff employees with administrative titles live within the 
Coachella city limits. 
The information you have requested does 
not fall under any provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
ttActtt ),l which is the body of law that is administered by the Fair 
Political Practices commission. 
Under the Act, public officials, which includes designated 
employees of local governmental agencies, are required to complete 
an annual statement of economic interests. 
This document only 
requires that the employee give the name and mailing address of 
the employing agency. 
The employee does not disclose his or her 
home mailing address. 
I trust this answers your question. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: 
/\1 i l 14r1.~U 
Ji~rR~CHer 
Counsel, Legal Division 
KED:JRS:plh 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
--- - "" ~ 


Sept. 30, 
California Fair Political Practices Commission 
City Attorney, City of Coachella 
Corky Larson, Riverside County Supervisor 
Steve Clute, Assemblyman, 68th Disttict 
Mr. 
Ne~on, Coachella City Manager 
YOlanda Coba, Coachella City Mayor 
,498 
UCT 
I went to see Linda Garza and requested to see the Coachella 
City Manager, Mr. Nelson, and asked him to simply tell me how many 
Coachella City staff employees with Administrative Titles live 
within the Coachella City limits. 
He said, no problem, lets go 
see Linda Garza and ask her to supply you with this information. 
We went to see Linda Garza, Mr. Nelson told her to give me the 
information I requested. 
I told Linda that I didn't want names 
or addresses just figuresjjust figures. 
She appeared to be busy 
at this time so I asked her to mail the information to me before 
that day was over. 
Later on the day she called and left me a 
message for me to contact her. 
I returned the call and spoke to 
her and she indicated that the information I requested was illegal 
according to the City Attorney, therefore, she was denying my 
request. 
My reply to her was that I was under the impression that 
that since 
I didn't ask for names or addresses that it was 
public information. 
Her reply was, "I don't know about that Federico". 
Then I requested from her a written denial be sent to my home address. 
The phone conversion was over. 
The following day, Mrs. Garza called 
informing me that my request for this particular information had 
to be in written form. 
We agreed that we would make this request 
from her. 
The question I pose is this: 
Am I requesting for something 
that is not appropriate? 
request has been denied? 
Or, Is there a special reason why my 
Or, Is there something hore that shouldn't 
be known by the Coachella citizens? 
.interesting! 
Again, my question is simply this, how many of the Coachella 
City Administrative staff live within the city limits; 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Federico Murillo 
52-256 Calle Camacho 
Coachella, CA 
92236 
Dear Mr. Murillo: 
October 17, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-584 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on October 5, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Jill Stecher an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will cont~ct you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt ofa proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
i 
Kathryn(~. Donovan 
, J 
General Counsel 
KED:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question: Am I requesting for something that is not appropriate? Or, Is there a special reason why my request has been denied? Or, Is there something more that shouldn't be known by the Coachella citizens? Again, my question is simply this, how many of the Coachella City Administrative staff live within the city limits;
sections.conclusion: The information you have requested does not fall under any provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"), which is the body of law that is administered by the Fair Political Practices commission. Under the Act, public officials, which includes designated employees of local governmental agencies, are required to complete an annual statement of economic interests. This document only requires that the employee give the name and mailing address of the employing agency. The employee does not disclose his or her home mailing address.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Political Reform Act require or allow disclosure of how many Coachella City staff employees with administrative titles live within city limits, and is the City's denial of this information request legally justified?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the requested information does not fall under the Political Reform Act, as employee statements of economic interests only require disclosure of the employing agency's mailing address, not employees' home addresses or residential locations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (September 30, 1989 letter from Murillo with detailed narrative) and the FPPC informal advice response (November 21, 1989 letter from Kathryn E. Donovan). Also includes a preliminary acknowledgment letter from October 17, 1989. Extracted information primarily from the final advice letter which contains the FPPC's formal response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Am I requesting for something that is not appropriate? Or, Is there a special reason why my request has been denied? Or, Is there something more that shouldn't be known by the Coachella citizens? Again, my question is simply this, how many of the Coachella City Administrative staff live within the city limits;

CONCLUSION: The information you have requested does not fall under any provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"), which is the body of law that is administered by the Fair Political Practices commission. Under the Act, public officials, which includes designated employees of local governmental agencies, are required to complete an annual statement of economic interests. This document only requires that the employee give the name and mailing address of the employing agency. The employee does not disclose his or her home mailing address.

embedding.summary: Federico Murillo requested FPPC advice regarding whether information about how many Coachella City administrative staff live within city limits is public information or protected under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC determined the requested information does not fall under the Political Reform Act provisions, as employee statements of economic interests only require disclosure of the employing agency's address, not home addresses.
